# Kuma — Master Build Status

**Audited:** 2026-07-23 · **Baseline:** `a21f956` · **Principles:** `docs/PRINCIPLES.md`

**Mission.** Kuma — your AI Software Healing Engineer. Six specialist agents review every pull request, verify each finding against the real diff, and propose fixes a human approves.

Single source of truth for what remains to be built. The /build-status page reads this file; the editor route rewrites it; agents read it before starting work. Items are sorted by (stage, id) so parallel lanes merge as item additions rather than whole-file conflicts. A duplicate id is a merge signal, not an append.

## Principles (abridged — canon is the principles doc)

- Honesty is the product. Never fabricate data, status or a passing result; a control that cannot act is disabled with its reason, never a live-looking button.
- The human-in-the-loop moat. Nothing merges or posts autonomously — only a human approves, only a human sends.
- Tenancy is a security boundary. Every query scoped by installationId; one resolver, one truth; never an unscoped DELETE on tenant data.
- Bring your own model. Every tenant action rides their key — encrypted at rest, never logged, never returned, never destroyed as a side effect.
- prisma migrate deploy, never db push. All worktrees share one Postgres.
- Characterization tests first; a refactor commit changes no behaviour and a fix commit moves no code.
- Verify by driving the real flow, not only a green suite.

## Where the product stands today

_One row per user-facing surface. A surface with a gap names the work items that close it, so this section is a rollup and never a second copy of the work._

- [ ] **Agents** — `surface-agents` (P1 · Open · M)

  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md (recorded by build-status-map)

## Honesty & security debt

_While any of these is open the product asserts something about itself that is not true, or leaves a credential unguarded. These come before features._

- [ ] **Collector config commits plaintext ClickHouse credentials** — `collector-plaintext-credentials` (P0 · Open · S)
  - Gap: otel-collector-config.yaml commits `superlog/superlog` in the clear. Fine for local compose; it must not survive into any deploy path, and nothing currently stops it.
  - Evidence: `infra otel-collector-config.yaml`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **MCP tokens are plaintext and the code exchange is simulated** — `mcp-token-plaintext-and-simulated-oauth` (P0 · Open · M)
  - Works: Refresh-on-expiry now exists and fails closed; the creds file is written 0o600.
  - Gap: mcp/auth.py:90 never performs a real code exchange — it fabricates simulated_token_for_<code>, so there is no expires_in to store even where the schema has a slot. Tokens persist as JSON on disk. _save_config's mkdir does not retroactively tighten a pre-existing loose .agents/ directory. Decide: give MCP a real OAuth exchange, or remove the surface until it has one.
  - Evidence: `packages/agents/src/arete_agents/mcp/auth.py:90, mcp/manager.py`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Phase 2b item 1, Phase 4 items 6-7) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **Prose-shaped credentials still reach sinks** — `prose-credentials-reach-sinks` (P0 · Open · M)
  - Works: Shaped secrets (sk-*, gh?_*, Bearer, key-ish URL params) and blocklisted object keys are scrubbed; URL query stripping now fires on embedded URLs anywhere in a string.
  - Gap: A memory body containing a free-text credential matches no shape and no object key, and has no URL to strip. Verified stored verbatim. Fixing it means amending the frozen §5 pattern set, with real false-positive risk on ordinary prose — a decision, not a patch. Applies to every sink equally.
  - Evidence: `scrubText / scrubSinkText, SECRET_VALUE_PATTERNS`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Phase 2b item 6) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **SecurityAssessor returns fabricated findings** — `security-assessor-fabricates` (P0 · Open · M)
  - Gap: Returns simulated results, string-matched off the skill filename — the last live fabrication in packages/agents, and a direct violation of the honesty rule that is the product's differentiator. Either make it real or make it refuse. It must never reach the UI as-is.
  - Evidence: `packages/agents/src/arete_agents/skills/security.py:12`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §4 B7 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **SSE endpoint has no auth or tenant scoping, and CORS is *** — `sse-endpoint-unauthenticated` (P0 · Open · M)
  - Gap: Flagged during Lane A planning and never fixed. Needs a hardening item before any non-local deployment.
  - Evidence: `packages/webhook/src/sse-handler.ts`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Discovered / unscheduled) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **INTERNAL_API_TOKEN has no expiry — and expiry is not expressible** — `internal-token-no-expiry` (P0 · Open · L)
  - Works: One static shared secret authenticates every internal service-to-service call, and it does authenticate correctly.
  - Gap: No exp, no iat, no rotation, no revocation — and tokenMatches(header, token) takes no clock argument, so expiry is not merely absent but not expressible in the current code path. It guards every write-and-spend route on both the webhook and agents services. Needs a credential-lifecycle design (rotation window, dual-token overlap so rotation cannot break in-flight calls), not a patch. Resolve BEFORE building more dashboard proxies onto it.
  - Evidence: `packages/webhook/src/internal-auth.ts:43`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Phase 2b item 1) (recorded by backlog)

## Make the built product reachable

_Capability that is already built, tested and merged, that no human can reach. Highest value per unit risk; mostly S/M._

- [x] **Approve a proposed fix** — `approve-a-proposed-fix` (P1 · Shipped · S)
  - Works: The gate renders on the Services work-item panel, reading the container's stored state. No /agents URL was created, so this is a step toward the Stage 2 absorption rather than against it.
  - Evidence: `1192d37`
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 1.1 (recorded by nautilus)
- [x] **Approvals panel — the safety gate is visible, and rejectable** — `approvals-panel` (P1 · Shipped · M)
  - Works: Session-scoped approve proxy plus a genuinely new reject route. 'No' was not an answer a human could give before — executeApproval's REJECTED branch was dead code. Tenancy proven live: a prompt under a different installation did not appear.
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 1.3 (recorded by nautilus)
- [x] **Post the approved PR** — `post-the-approved-pr` (P1 · Shipped · M)
  - Works: Same panel as the approve gate; the dead !realMode branch is bypassed rather than revived.
  - Evidence: `1192d37`
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 1.2 (recorded by nautilus)
- [x] **Silence / un-silence a finding** — `silence-a-finding` (P1 · Shipped · M)
  - Works: POST /api/findings/[id]/noise is the dashboard's only noiseState writer. A human may assert only OPEN and SILENCED; UNDER_OBSERVATION and ESCALATED are machine-derived and render read-only, because a button asserting one would claim an observation that never happened.
  - Gap: Known limitation, recorded not fixed: restoring returns a finding to OPEN, never to the machine state that preceded the silence — that prior state is stored nowhere, and inventing one would be fabricated status.
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 1.4 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Agent memory has no surface** — `agent-memory-ui` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Works: Kuma really does record what it learns per repository and injects it into later reviews.
  - Gap: Nothing displays it. Belongs on repository settings as 'What Kuma has learned', with an archive action.
  - Blocked by: agent-memory-cap-and-archive
  - Evidence: `packages/webhook/src/memory-write.ts:104,248`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §3 A3 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Live throughput metrics — the dark wire** — `live-throughput-metrics` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Works: The worker publishes review-lifecycle metrics to a server-sent stream.
  - Gap: No consumer — the source comments call it a dark wire. An Overview throughput tile needs a server-side proxy, which depends on the internal-token lifecycle decision.
  - Blocked by: internal-token-no-expiry
  - Evidence: `packages/webhook/src/agent-metrics.ts:4`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §3 A4 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Connect Sentry** — `connect-sentry` (P2 · Blocked · S)
  - Works: The connector and its tests are fully built. Flipping connector-catalog.ts status:'planned' is the whole change.
  - Gap: Blocked on Sentry's own integration approval, not on us.
  - Evidence: `connector-catalog.ts`
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 1.5 (recorded by nautilus)

## Agents become a layer inside Services

_The locked user decision: one place to see what Kuma is doing to my services, not two._

- [ ] **Decide the fate of the Agents-rail work inbox (built backwards)** — `revert-or-subsume-agents-rail-inbox` (P1 · Open · S)
  - Gap: 73e2040 put the Services work inbox INTO the Agents rail — the reverse of the locked decision. It entrenches two pages instead of consolidating to one, and duplicates a section onto a page scheduled for absorption. Small, additive, tested and harmless while /agents exists, but it points the wrong way. Revert it, or let the absorption subsume it — not yet decided.
  - Evidence: `73e2040`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-ridley-closeout.md (recorded by ridley-closeout)
- [ ] **Agent config changes are never saved** — `agent-config-persistence` (P1 · Open · M)
  - Gap: Every parameter control in the config drawer is local-only and explicitly 'deliberately NOT persisted'. Without this, the Stage 2 agents layer ships parameter controls that are theatre — so either build it, or give them an explicit disabled state.
  - Evidence: `agent-config-drawer.tsx:24,245`
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 2.4 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Agents layer inside Services** — `agents-layer-inside-services` (P1 · Open · L)
  - Works: The locked decision, refined by the user 2026-07-22: agents are an additional layer within Services showing how they interact with the workflow; select the agents that are working to open their conversation and their parameter controls.
  - Gap: Not started. Spec §0/§1 is locked; the wider Investigations slice around it is not greenlit.
  - Blocked by: agent-config-persistence
  - Evidence: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-22-investigations-surface-and-agent-harness-design.md §0/§1`
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 2.2 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Retire /agents as a nav destination** — `retire-agents-nav` (P2 · Open · S)
  - Gap: Keep the route for deep-links; remove the tab once the absorption lands.
  - Blocked by: agents-layer-inside-services
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 2.3 (recorded by nautilus)

## Papercuts and the refresh

_Small, visible, and each one is a moment where the product currently feels unfinished._

- [ ] **Fix / Dismiss stops full-page reloading** — `fix-dismiss-no-full-reload` (P2 · Open · S)
  - Gap: window.location.reload() is the only feedback, and it loses rail expansion, selection and stream position.
  - Evidence: `services-workspace.tsx`
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 3.4 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Scan completion replaces the blind timer** — `scan-completion-signal` (P2 · Open · S)
  - Gap: setTimeout(reload, 1500) is unconnected to scan completion — a slower scan reloads into stale state and looks like it did nothing.
  - Evidence: `services-workspace.tsx`
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 3.3 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Kuma logo becomes a true global refresh** — `global-refresh-kuma-logo` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Works: The logo already reflects real global loading state.
  - Gap: Clicking it should re-pull services, connections, telemetry and scan state, turning the existing spin into honest progress. It must show real completion per source, and a source that fails must say so — a spin that resolves on a timer would be exactly the fabricated status the honesty rules forbid.
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 3.1 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Manual investigations can start a fix** — `manual-investigation-start-fix` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Gap: The alert path (receiver.ts to routeIncidentToFix) creates a WorkItem, container and fix drive. The UI path writes an Incident and never routes it, so a hand-opened investigation is a dead end that can only be marked noise. Preserve the human approve then send gate.
  - Evidence: `lib/incidents.ts:211 createManualIncident`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §4 B1 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Delete synth-ledger.tsx (zero importers)** — `delete-synth-ledger` (P3 · Open · S)
  - Gap: Exported, imported by nothing.
  - Evidence: `synth-ledger.tsx`

- [ ] **Two links that point at the wrong place** — `back-to-overview-and-sample-data-copy` (P3 · Open · S)
  - Gap: 'Back to Overview' goes to / (the marketing root), not /overview. 'Explore with sample data' leads to a page that never shows sample data — stale copy that should be removed rather than made true.
  - Evidence: `reviews/[id]/page.tsx:96, connections/[id]/page.tsx:129-131`

## Hygiene that keeps the map honest

_Correctness bugs and stale records. Cheap individually; collectively they decide whether anyone can trust the docs._

- [ ] **review-pr-heavy queue has a producer and no consumer** — `review-pr-heavy-no-consumer` (P1 · Open · S)
  - Gap: PRs with more than 50 changed files are routed to a queue nothing consumes — worker.ts starts a Worker only on the fast queue. Those reviews never run and their traces dead-end. Pre-existing, but it means large PRs silently get no review at all.
  - Evidence: `webhook-handler.ts:114, backfill.ts:86, worker.ts:311`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Phase 1 final review) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **A locked-out user has no recovery path** — `password-reset-and-email-verification` (P1 · Open · M)
  - Gap: User.emailVerified exists in the schema and is never written. There is no password reset and no email verification, so a user who loses their password loses their account.
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 4.4 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Agent memory freezes at 20 rows forever** — `agent-memory-cap-and-archive` (P1 · Open · M)
  - Gap: Nothing in the repo ever sets status='archived', and the read cap and write cap are the same number — so once a repo reaches 20 ACTIVE rows every later write returns cap_exceeded forever and the memory set is frozen at whatever it first learned. Separately the cap is check-then-create with no transaction, so concurrent writes can all pass it. The mechanism exists; the missing piece is the policy (age out? evict least-cited? let a human archive?) and a surface to apply it.
  - Evidence: `packages/webhook/src/memory-write.ts`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (agent memory sink) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **Adopt getAccountState on the last two ad-hoc pages** — `adopt-account-state-agents-map` (P2 · Open · S)
  - Gap: agents/page.tsx and map/page.tsx are the last ad-hoc hasAccess users. The account-state contract says every surface derives from the one resolver.
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 4.3 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Correct the tracking docs that plans are drawn from** — `correct-tracking-docs` (P2 · Open · S)
  - Gap: backlog.md records 7 entries open that are closed in code, and build-status-map.md §3 A5 / §4 B2, B8 went stale when the retry worker and internal-token work landed. Every plan is drawn off these files, so a stale entry propagates into the next plan.
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 4.1-4.2 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **The send route never advances the work item to posted** — `send-route-work-item-posted` (P2 · Open · S)
  - Gap: approve moves fixing to staged, but after a successful send the container becomes posted while its work item stays staged. Not visible locally because STAGING_SERVICE_URL is unset and send honestly 503s.
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 1 recorded findings (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Unguarded second checks.update re-opens the double-retry** — `checks-update-double-retry` (P2 · Open · S)
  - Gap: On BOTH worker paths, the checks.update inside the publish-failure catch is itself unguarded. If it throws (transient GitHub failure while reporting a degraded outcome) the error escapes the catch, the job re-throws, and BullMQ retries the whole pipeline despite it having already produced a usable result — the exact double-retry the two-block split was meant to prevent, one call later.
  - Evidence: `packages/webhook/src/worker.ts:134-144, 239-249`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Deferred from Phase 4, item 4) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **Confidence is invisible on pull-request findings** — `confidence-on-review-findings` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Works: Confidence is computed and shown on scan and fix work items.
  - Gap: ReviewComment has no confidence column at all, so PR findings show none. Schema column plus UI.
  - Evidence: `schema.prisma (ReviewComment)`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §4 B3 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Decide the Python fingerprint question** — `python-fingerprint-decision` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Gap: Four record_exception sites in packages/agents need the fingerprint attribute; Python's API accepts attributes=, so the mechanical part is trivial. The blocker is that it needs nine ordered regexes ported — a second implementation, which contract §5 forbids. A drifting copy would split arete-agents errors into two groups with no test able to catch it from either side. Honest options: a shared normalization service, a generated port with golden vectors asserted from both languages, or an explicit spec amendment accepting the copy. A second hand-written copy is forbidden.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-ridley-closeout.md (Open, item 1) (recorded by ridley-closeout)
- [ ] **Trend charts are off by one day for most timezones** — `trends-utc-vs-local-day` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Gap: lib/trends.ts buckets by LOCAL calendar day while the ClickHouse window is UTC. Every chart in the product is affected for any viewer whose timezone crosses midnight relative to UTC, so the fix needs its own change and its own verification.
  - Evidence: `packages/dashboard/src/lib/trends.ts`
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 1 recorded findings (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **The pino log path is unstamped with issue fingerprints** — `pino-log-path-unstamped` (P3 · Open · S)
  - Gap: otel_exceptions_from_logs_mv and issue_activity_daily read LogAttributes['superlog.issue_fingerprint']; only the span lane is stamped so far, so the log half of those projections cannot group.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-ridley-closeout.md (Open, item 2) (recorded by ridley-closeout)
- [ ] **Tracing coverage gaps in CI and the webhook** — `otel-ci-and-tracing-coverage` (P3 · Open · S)
  - Gap: Webhook /health is not excluded from tracing (the agents' is), so a 5s probe will dominate span volume. CI smoke-boots dist/otel.js and dist/index.js but never dist/otel-worker.js — the boot path that owns service.name=arete-worker and the whole review span tree is unsmoked.
  - Evidence: `.github/workflows/ci.yml:91, packages/agents/.../server.py:52`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Phase 1 final review) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **Span names and metrics drift from the spec** — `otel-span-conventions-drift` (P3 · Open · M)
  - Gap: The Python lane emits agent_review:{name} / synthesize_reviews instead of spec §5's agent.review (attr agent.role) / review.synthesize, and no llm.generate span exists. arete.agent.duration has a histogram View registered in both lanes and documented in the skill, but no instrument ever records it. review.publish is wrapped on the PR path and absent on the check_run path. Either rename/record, or amend §5 to match reality — and keep debug-from-telemetry in sync.
  - Evidence: `orchestrator.py:393,456; init.ts:64; observability.py:152; worker.ts`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Phase 1 final review, Phase 4 item 2) (recorded by backlog)

## The bigger bets

_Week-scale work. Two of these specs were never greenlit — confirm intent before spending the time._

- [ ] **Outbound webhook management API + Settings UI** — `outbound-webhook-management-ui` (P1 · Open · M)
  - Works: Signing, retry backoff with rescheduling, SSRF-guarded delivery and a delivery log all work, and it already fires on every persisted review. The retry worker now runs.
  - Gap: The management API is deliberately unmounted, so nobody can register an endpoint or see a delivery, and nothing surfaces delivery health — you cannot see that a delivery failed, how many attempts remain, or the last error. This is the single unblock for every Slack/Linear/PagerDuty relay.
  - Evidence: `packages/webhook/src/server.ts:408-414, persistence.ts:239-256`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §3 A2 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Docker sandbox workspace agent — run the PR's tests for real** — `docker-sandbox-workspace-agent` (P2 · Open · L)
  - Gap: The only genuinely missing plan artifact in the repo (workspace.py). Of ~380 symbols declared across 48 plans, this is the one that was never written. It blocks the work-floor Phase B agents.
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 5.4 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Glass Box in production** — `glass-box-production` (P2 · Blocked · L)
  - Works: The SSE feed, provenance banner and auto-refresh work as a dev sidecar.
  - Gap: Structurally undeployable without an installationId filter, and inert in production by design today. Spec is awaiting approval — do not build before it is greenlit.
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 5.2 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Investigations surface + tool-calling fix harness** — `investigations-surface-and-fix-harness` (P2 · Blocked · L)
  - Gap: Spec is proposed and awaiting user review — confirm intent before spending a week. Includes giving the fix pipeline a tool loop: fix_pipeline.py makes one direct LLM call for a JSON blob, so spec §3's 'tool descriptions ARE the prompt' cannot apply to healing and rubrics necessarily live in the prompt.
  - Evidence: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-22-investigations-surface-and-agent-harness-design.md`
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 5.1 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Work-floor Phase B agents** — `work-floor-phase-b-agents` (P3 · Blocked · L)
  - Gap: Repro / root-cause / fix-author / test-author / QA agents. Blocked on the Docker sandbox workspace agent.
  - Blocked by: docker-sandbox-workspace-agent
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md Stage 5.3 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Tenant telemetry ingestion platform** — `tenant-telemetry-ingest` (P3 · Deferred · L)
  - Gap: Two-tier ingest edge, ingest keys, strip-then-stamp tenancy, deterministic fingerprint grouping, rollups, background poller and snapshot history, per-tenant retention and a prompt-content consent model. Deliberately deferred — the current stack observes Kuma itself, not the customer's services. Needs a product decision, not code.
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Later — Phase 3) (recorded by backlog)

## Onboarding & install

_The Connect Workspace lane: get Kuma into the user's actual codebase. Designed 2026-07-20, never built. HARD CONSTRAINT on every item: extend the existing setup, do not greenfield._

- [ ] **Real account-state signals for every new onboarding step** — `account-state-signals-for-new-steps` (P1 · Open · M)
  - Gap: Each new checklist step (workspace connected, install complete, MCP connected) needs a real getAccountState signal before it may show a checkmark. No fabricated ticks. Existing users must land on the correct new step rather than being reset to the start.
  - Evidence: `packages/dashboard/src/lib/overview-setup.ts, lib/account-state.ts`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **The prompt to agent to MCP/skill setup workflow** — `setup-prompt-workflow` (P1 · Open · M)
  - Gap: The setup prompt itself — what Kuma hands the user's coding agent to run, and how the result is verified back. Named as a 'coming soon' sub-step on the onboarding checklist with nothing behind it. The user holds reference data on how this is done.
  - Evidence: `lib/overview-setup.ts:76-84`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (Immediate next, item 1) (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Connect Workspace — clone a repo or select a local folder** — `connect-workspace-clone-or-folder` (P1 · Open · L)
  - Gap: After the GitHub connect step, the user should be able to clone a repo URL OR point at a local folder, so Kuma has the actual working tree it needs for code and telemetry setup. Today this survives only as an honest 'coming soon' sub-step. HARD CONSTRAINT: extend the existing setup — reconcile with GitHub App connect, model connections, and the Sensorium/context-map indexing that already runs on install. Do not greenfield.
  - Evidence: `lib/overview-setup.ts:76-84`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (Immediate next, item 1) (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Kuma install — skills, code mapping, telemetry wiring** — `kuma-install-skills-and-codemap` (P1 · Open · L)
  - Gap: Connecting a coding agent (Claude Code recommended) should kick off the Kuma install into the user's repo: the Kuma skills, the code graph embedded in the project, and OpenTelemetry wired up. The pieces exist separately (agents skills/loader, the context-map checkout and graph export that already run on install) — this is the flow that composes them.
  - Evidence: `packages/agents/src/arete_agents/skills/loader.py, context_map/graph_export.py`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (Immediate next, item 1) (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **MCP setup runnable from an agent here, not the IDE** — `mcp-setup-from-agent` (P1 · Open · L)
  - Gap: The setup should run from an agent inside Kuma rather than requiring the user to do it in their IDE. Depends on the MCP surface being real — today there is no dashboard surface to add or list MCP servers, and the OAuth exchange is simulated.
  - Blocked by: mcp-token-plaintext-and-simulated-oauth, mcp-servers-no-surface
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (Immediate next, item 1) (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **MCP servers are consume-only and CLI-only** — `mcp-servers-no-surface` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Works: Kuma can consume third-party MCP servers, driven from the CLI.
  - Gap: No dashboard surface to add or list them.
  - Evidence: `packages/agents/src/arete_agents/mcp/manager.py`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §4 B6 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Kuma as an MCP server (the inversion)** — `kuma-as-mcp-server` (P2 · Open · L)
  - Gap: Expose Kuma's findings and review judgment back into the user's coding agent. Only the client half exists today — Kuma can consume third-party MCP servers, but nothing can consume Kuma. This is the 'Connect MCP Kuma to your agent' checklist sub-step.
  - Evidence: `lib/overview-setup.ts:103-107, packages/agents/.../mcp/manager.py`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §5 C4 (recorded by build-status-map)

## SDLC & ship it

_What stands between a dogfood server and a product other people can use._

- [ ] **Deploy pipeline, hosted Postgres, production runbook** — `deploy-pipeline-and-prod-runbook` (P1 · Open · L)
  - Gap: There is no deploy path. Everything runs on a local dogfood server against a shared local Postgres. Needs a pipeline, a hosted database, and a runbook for the three services (dashboard, webhook, agents).
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (Immediate next, item 5) (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Feature commits keep bypassing the integration gate** — `lane-to-preview-governance` (P2 · Open · S)
  - Gap: Commits have repeatedly landed directly on a serving branch instead of going lane to integration-preview to a reviewed merge, each time requiring a reverse-merge and suite repair. Duplicate parallel work has also shipped three times (agent-statuses, the healing loop, model switching). A process gap, not a code gap — but it costs real hours.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (Manual intervention, item 3) (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Four packages have no CI job** — `ci-for-untested-packages` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Gap: orchestration, topology, net-guard and db have test suites that CI never runs, so a regression in any of them lands green.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (Immediate next, item 5) (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **The Google OAuth client no longer exists** — `google-oauth-client-missing` (P2 · Blocked · S)
  - Gap: Deleted upstream in Google Cloud Console. Email/password login is the working path. Either recreate the client or remove the sign-in option rather than leaving a button that cannot succeed.
  - Source: docs/handoff/2026-07-22-orchestration-briefs.md §1 (recorded by orchestration-briefs)
- [ ] **Managed environment template** — `managed-env-template` (P3 · Open · S)
  - Gap: Env setup is hand-rolled per worktree. A template that a new environment can be provisioned from, covering OAuth, the internal token, encryption keys and service URLs.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **No single command runs every suite** — `root-aggregate-test-script` (P3 · Open · S)
  - Gap: Each package is invoked separately, which is how a red suite hid behind a green one before. One root script that runs everything and reports per-package.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (Immediate next, item 5) (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **Lint ratchets held at warn so CI can be green** — `typed-debt-ratchets` (P3 · Open · M)
  - Gap: packages/dashboard holds 51 no-explicit-any at warn; packages/webhook holds 78 no-console at warn and turns no-explicit-any off entirely at the Octokit boundary; packages/agents sets ruff line-length to 120. Each is a deliberate, documented ratchet — flip back to error once paid. Also descoped and never rescheduled: vitest/pytest coverage reporting, advisory only.
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Typed debt) (recorded by backlog)

## Product & commercial

_Revenue, integrations and the public surface. Sequenced last on purpose._

- [ ] **New Anthropic connections default to the slowest model** — `anthropic-catalog-default-tier` (P2 · Open · S)
  - Works: Fast tiers exist and the agent role defaults were re-tiered for speed (chat/quality/test_coverage/performance/ci = haiku, security/business_logic/deployment_safety/synthesizer = sonnet).
  - Gap: The connect UI still lists claude-opus-4-8 first, so a new connection gets the slowest model by default — and the BYO path runs ALL roles on the one model the user picked, so that choice is not softened by the role tiers.
  - Evidence: `packages/dashboard/src/lib/model-catalog.ts:56`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (Immediate next, item 3) (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **No self-serve upgrade** — `self-serve-upgrade-billing` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Works: Real billing usage is shown against the free-tier limit, and the absence of an upgrade path is stated plainly rather than faked.
  - Gap: A user who hits the limit cannot pay. Stripe groundwork exists in packages/webhook.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §1 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Review scope filters** — `review-scope-filters` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Gap: No way to say which paths Kuma should or should not review. Every PR is reviewed whole.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §5 C3 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Telemetry snapshots go stale with no indication** — `telemetry-freshness-poller` (P2 · Open · M)
  - Works: The grid reads a real telemetry snapshot per repository.
  - Gap: Snapshots are captured only during a review and there is no background poller, so a quiet repo shows arbitrarily stale data with nothing but fetchedAt to say so. TelemetrySnapshotRecord is a latest-value row, not a history table.
  - Evidence: `schema.prisma:367-374`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §4 B5 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **OpenTelemetry is 95% dark to the UI** — `tracing-and-log-search` (P2 · Open · L)
  - Works: Full OpenTelemetry instrumentation flows into ClickHouse and Jaeger, and Jaeger trace deep-links exist.
  - Gap: Exactly one metric reaches the UI. No trace view, no span drill-down, no log search. This is what blocks telemetry-fed investigations — the healing agent cannot read an incident's own trace context.
  - Evidence: `packages/dashboard/src/lib/queries.ts getAgentEventsPerMinute`
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §4 B2 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Slack / Linear / PagerDuty relays** — `relays-slack-linear-pagerduty` (P2 · Blocked · M)
  - Gap: Each relay is a thin consumer once outbound webhook management lands. The retry worker is already running, so this is the last dependency.
  - Blocked by: outbound-webhook-management-ui
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §5 C1 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Review.prUrl + resolve / noise reason codes** — `review-pr-url-and-reason-codes` (P3 · Open · S)
  - Gap: A review does not store a link back to its pull request, and resolving or silencing a finding records no reason.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §5 C5 (recorded by build-status-map)
- [ ] **Public API & API keys** — `public-api-and-keys` (P3 · Open · L)
  - Gap: No key issuance, no read or management REST surface.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-build-status-map.md §5 C2 (recorded by build-status-map)

## Not schedulable yet

_Blocked on a funded API key, production volume, aged data, or someone else's approval. Parked with the reason — never quietly dropped, never dressed up as a plan._

- [ ] **The Anthropic account is at $0** — `parked-anthropic-zero-balance` (P0 · Not schedulable · S)
  - Gap: Not a code problem and not fixable from here. Agent work runs on local Ollama until credits are added. Several other parked items are gated on this, because they need a funded key to measure. A lost provider key must be regenerated — providers do not re-issue them.
  - Source: docs/handoff/2026-07-22-orchestration-briefs.md §1 (recorded by orchestration-briefs)
- [ ] **Is haiku adequate to AUTHOR fixes?** — `parked-haiku-fix-authoring-adequacy` (P2 · Not schedulable · M)
  - Gap: Fix authoring reuses each dimension's configured REVIEW tier, and performance/quality/test_coverage default to haiku. Haiku's original job is critiquing a diff, not generating a complete file replacement. Do NOT change the tier blind: needs a funded key plus a regression corpus of known-fixable issues to measure haiku-vs-sonnet patch pass rate first.
  - Blocked by: parked-anthropic-zero-balance
  - Evidence: `fix_pipeline.py:313, config.py:49-51`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Deferred from Phase 3, item 1) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **Review job retries duplicate the per-agent retry** — `parked-review-job-double-retry` (P2 · Not schedulable · M)
  - Gap: processGitHubPullRequest re-throws any pipeline failure and BullMQ's attempts:3 then re-runs the ENTIRE multi-file multi-agent review from scratch, on top of each agent's own retry. Needs a real induced transient provider failure mid-review to count the actual duplicated calls. Likely fix once measured: distinguish 'some agents failed, partial result produced' from 'the pipeline crashed for an infra reason'.
  - Evidence: `packages/webhook/src/worker.ts:94-113, queue.ts:152-157`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (efficiency review) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **Review LLM fan-out is unbounded per review** — `parked-review-concurrency-tuning` (P2 · Not schedulable · M)
  - Gap: LangGraph fans out one node per (file x agent) pair. A 20-file PR reviewed by 6 agents is ~120 concurrent LLM calls for ONE review; five concurrent reviews is up to ~600 simultaneous provider calls. The real throughput bottleneck is likely the provider's rate limit, not REVIEW_QUEUE_CONCURRENCY=5. The max_concurrency default is also untuned. Both need a real large PR and a funded key to measure.
  - Blocked by: parked-anthropic-zero-balance
  - Evidence: `orchestrator.py:353-381`
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (efficiency review) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **Browser QA on the code-map reading panel** — `parked-code-map-browser-qa` (P3 · Not schedulable · S)
  - Gap: A real browser pass that a unit suite cannot stand in for. A human task, not an agent one.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-20-phase-wrap-up.md (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **ClickHouse TTLs are configured but unverified live** — `parked-clickhouse-ttl-verification` (P3 · Not schedulable · S)
  - Gap: 30d raw / 90d rollup with ttl_only_drop_parts=1 is correct on paper, but every row in this ClickHouse is hours old — nowhere near the retention window. Needs real aged data to confirm parts actually drop.
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (efficiency review) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **End-to-end ERROR-severity log path is unexercised** — `parked-error-severity-log` (P3 · Not schedulable · S)
  - Gap: otel_logs has zero rows at ERROR+ because the dashboard exports traces only. Blocked on such a row existing, not on code — the SQL, timestamp format and column projection are already verified against real rows at a lowered threshold.
  - Source: docs/status/2026-07-22-ridley-closeout.md (recorded by ridley-closeout)
- [ ] **Make Signals visibly render** — `parked-signals-visibly-render` (P3 · Not schedulable · S)
  - Gap: Needs a real Alertmanager alert. Synthetic seeds are forbidden — seeding one would be fabricated data on the exact surface whose job is to be trustworthy.
  - Source: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-23-reachability-and-consolidation-roadmap.md §3 (recorded by nautilus)
- [ ] **ReviewComment index choices** — `parked-review-comment-indexes` (P3 · Not schedulable · S)
  - Gap: May still be missing indexes on severity, category and noiseState. The reviewId index cut 25-66% off the four hottest queries at synthetic scale, but that number entangles the join index with these per-column filters. Measure each individually with EXPLAIN ANALYZE before adding any — do not guess.
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (efficiency review) (recorded by backlog)
- [ ] **MCP RFC 8414 discovery + dynamic client registration** — `parked-mcp-rfc8414-discovery` (P3 · Not schedulable · M)
  - Gap: Speculative until a real MCP server that hands out no static token_url needs it, and nothing in this environment validates such a flow.
  - Source: docs/roadmap/backlog.md (Deferred from Phase 4, item 1) (recorded by backlog)

---

68 of 85 items are actionable today; the rest are shipped, or parked with a stated reason.
